title: Product Catalogue with Upload & Rates Management - Frontend

requirements.summary: Build a Catalogue page displaying all products in a responsive grid with filter/sort controls by category
acceptanceCriteria: All products fetched from the backend are displayed in a card grid.
Each card shows product image, name, category, and price (₹).
Filtering by category (All, Men's Wear, Women's Wear, Kids' Wear, Traditional/Ethnic Wear) works correctly.
Layout is responsive across mobile, tablet, and desktop.
Visual theme matches the existing saffron/gold and maroon palette.

file_operations.description: Create Catalogue page component that fetches products using backend.getProducts() and displays them in a responsive grid. Include category filter buttons (All, Men's Wear, Women's Wear, Kids' Wear, Traditional/Ethnic Wear) that filter the displayed products. Each product card must show the image (using imageUrl field), name, category badge, and price formatted as ₹{price}. Use the generated placeholder images (frontend/public/assets/generated/mens-wear.dim_400x400.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/womens-wear.dim_400x400.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/kids-wear.dim_400x400.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/ethnic-wear.dim_400x400.png) as fallback images if imageUrl is empty. Apply warm saffron/gold and maroon theme colors consistent with existing design. Ensure responsive grid layout using Tailwind classes (grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4).

Create reusable ProductCard component that accepts a Product prop (matching the backend Product interface). Display product image with fallback handling, product name as heading, category as a styled badge (map category enum values to display labels: mensWear → Men's Wear, womensWear → Women's Wear, kidsWear → Kids' Wear, ethnicWear → Traditional/Ethnic Wear), and price formatted as ₹{price}. Style with card shadow, rounded corners, and hover effects using the saffron/gold and maroon color palette from the existing theme.

requirements.summary: Add an Admin Upload page with a form to add new products with client-side validation
acceptanceCriteria: Upload form is accessible from the navigation bar (e.g., 'Add Product' link).
All required fields are present: Name, Category, Price, Image URL, Description.
Submitting a valid form calls the backend and adds the product.
The catalogue reflects the newly uploaded product without a full page reload.
Validation errors are shown inline if required fields are empty or price is invalid.
Success confirmation message is displayed after a successful upload.

file_operations.description: Create AddProduct page component with a form containing fields: Product Name (text input), Category (dropdown with options: Men's Wear, Women's Wear, Kids' Wear, Traditional/Ethnic Wear mapping to Category enum values), Price in ₹ (numeric input), Image URL (text input), and Description (textarea). Implement client-side validation: all fields required, price must be a positive number. On form submission, call backend.addProduct(name, category, price, imageUrl, description) and show success message. Include error handling for failed submissions. After successful upload, optionally redirect to /catalogue or show a success message with a link to view the catalogue. Style form inputs and buttons consistently with the existing saffron/gold and maroon theme.

requirements.summary: Add navigation links to the Catalogue and Add Product pages in the existing responsive navigation bar
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Catalogue' link appears in the navigation bar.
Clicking the link navigates to the Catalogue page.
The navigation bar remains responsive and correctly styled on all screen sizes.

file_operations.description: Add route definitions for /catalogue (rendering Catalogue page) and /add-product (rendering AddProduct page) to the React Router configuration. Import the Catalogue and AddProduct page components. Ensure routes are properly registered in the Routes component.

Add two new navigation links to the existing navigation bar: 'Catalogue' linking to /catalogue and 'Add Product' linking to /add-product. Place them alongside the existing Home, Collections, About, and Contact links. Ensure the navigation bar remains responsive and styled consistently with the existing saffron/gold and maroon theme on all screen sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop). The mobile menu should include the new links as well.
